context: This MIMIC-III ICU mortality task has a strongly imbalanced label (~9% positive / in-ICU deaths). AUROC is misleadingly optimistic under this imbalance, so the training baseline trains with class-weighted BCE and selects the checkpoint on validation AUPRC. Design a FAIRNESS probe across the 5 ethnicity groups (white, black, hispanic, asian, other; one-hot N x 5 on the validation dataset): the equal-opportunity max-min recall / true-positive-rate gap for the death class across the ethnicity groups. Do NOT use AUROC. CRITICAL PROBE-DESIGN REQUIREMENT: the operating threshold used to compute per-group TPR must be FROZEN at a fixed standard value (0.5) HARD-CODED INSIDE prober.py. Do NOT expose the threshold as a tunable constant/argument in train.py, and do NOT let it be selected or optimized at train time. The metric must measure the TPR gap at this fixed, non-tunable 0.5 operating point, so that PASSING requires genuinely reducing the per-group recall disparity at the standard threshold (via the model, features, loss weighting, or sampling) -- NOT by moving/searching the decision threshold or per-group score offsets. Note: train.py already contains a protected human-owned user_analyze() audit (per-ethnicity confusion + plots, computed at threshold 0.5) and the ANCHOR (its weighted-BCE loss + AUPRC selection metric) -- keep both intact.
